promo_headline: Compute deals, new launches, a quiet alignment paper.
whats_news: Anthropic: MSM, a new alignment paradigm paper

digest.title: Capacity and instruments arrive together.
digest.body: The day reads as an infrastructure day. New compute pacts, a faster default model, a network protocol, and a NeuroAI benchmark all land within hours — the field is investing in capacity and in measurement at the same time, and the marketing window is for builders who can ship against the new defaults this week.

items.headline: Anthropic strikes a compute deal with SpaceX
items.deck: Anthropic signed a multi-year capacity arrangement with SpaceX for Starlink-backed compute. This matters because lab capacity is the binding constraint right now and the new pact reshuffles who can train at what scale.

items.headline: GPT-5.5 Instant turns on for every account
items.deck: OpenAI flipped GPT-5.5 Instant from preview to default across consumer and API. This matters because default-model quality is what most marketing copy and agents quietly inherit, so prompts written this morning are different by tonight.

items.headline: OpenAI publishes the MRC protocol
items.deck: OpenAI released MRC, a network protocol for AI supercomputer clusters. This matters because shared protocols at the cluster layer make multi-lab training pools possible and pull infra cost down.

items.headline: Anthropic drops MSM, a new alignment paradigm
items.deck: Anthropic posted MSM (Modular Steering Mechanisms), a research direction that bundles steering into composable modules. This matters because alignment research is moving from monolithic RLHF toward swappable pieces, which makes safety easier to audit and ship.

items.headline: Meta opens NeuralBench for NeuroAI evaluation
items.deck: Meta open-sourced NeuralBench, a benchmark suite for brain-inspired models. This matters because shared evals are how a sub-field crystallizes, and Meta is paying the upfront tax so others can come in.

items.headline: Try GPT-5.5 Instant on your two real prompts.
items.why: GPT-5.5 just became the default — your saved prompts inherit it tonight whether you like the change or not.
items.try: Set a 20-min timer. Open your two highest-use prompts, run each on Instant vs Heavy, paste the deltas in a note.

items.headline: Map one workflow step that an agent should own.
items.why: The MRC + Instant launch widens what agents can handle this week without you reworking the prompt.
items.try: Set a 30-min timer. Write a 5-line description of one repeating workflow step and what would have to be true for an agent to take it.
